content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/README.md", "content": "# API Tests

Shell-based end-to-end tests that exercise the live HTTP surface of the
Scholarly backend. Every script sources `_common.sh` for shared helpers
(`login_as`, `expect_status`, `json_field`, `pass`, `fail`) and uses the
seed users that are bootstrapped on first `docker compose up`.

## Prerequisites

The compose stack must be running before any test script is executed:

```bash
docker compose up -d
```

Wait for the backend health check to return `{"status":"ok"}` before proceeding:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health
```

## Running the full API suite

The recommended entrypoint is `run_tests.sh` at the repository root. It
runs unit tests, cargo integration tests, frontend tests, **and** this
API suite in one command, with pass/fail counters and automatic skip if
the backend is unreachable:

```bash
# From the repo root
./run_tests.sh
```

To run only the API test scripts (skip all cargo tests):

```bash
./run_tests.sh --api-only
```

`run_tests.sh` discovers every non-helper script in this directory
(anything not prefixed with `_`) and runs them in alphabetical order.

## Running a single script

Execute any script directly from the repo root. The `BACKEND_URL`
environment variable controls which server is targeted (default:
`http://localhost:8000`):

```bash
# Default target (localhost:8000)
bash API_tests/health_check.sh

# Custom target
BACKEND_URL=http://staging.internal:8000 bash API_tests/health_check.sh
```

Scripts are self-contained: each one logs in, runs its assertions, and
prints `ALL PASS` on success or exits non-zero on the first failure.

## BACKEND_URL

All scripts inherit `BACKEND_URL` from the environment. `_common.sh`
derives `BASE="${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1"` from it, so every `curl` call
in every script automatically targets the right host.

| Scenario | Command prefix |
|---|---|
| Local compose stack (default) | *(no prefix needed)* |
| Custom port | `BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:9000` |
| Remote/staging host | `BACKEND_URL=http://host:8000` |

## Test scripts

| Script | Area covered |
|---|---|
| `health_check.sh` | Liveness probe (`GET /health`) |
| `auth_login_success.sh` | Successful login, token issuance |
| `auth_login_bad_password.sh` | Rejected credentials → 401 |
| `auth_lockout.sh` | Account lockout after repeated failures |
| `auth_unauthenticated_access.sh` | Protected routes reject missing token → 401 |
| `logout_revokes_session.sh` | Token unusable after logout |
| `admin_endpoint_protection.sh` | Non-admin blocked on admin-only routes → 403 |
| `admin_config.sh` | Admin config read/write |
| `admin_config_write.sh` | Config value mutation and audit trail |
| `user_crud.sh` | User create / update / deactivate (admin-only) |
| `audit_log_search.sh` | Audit log search filters and pagination |
| `audit_log_search_and_chain.sh` | Chain verification endpoint |
| `audit_log_export.sh` | CSV export: headers, content, capability gate |
| `library_journal_happy_path.sh` | Journal draft → approve → publish |
| `library_journal_validation.sh` | Invalid journal input → 422 |
| `library_journal_unauthorized.sh` | Journal writes rejected for non-librarian |
| `library_journal_not_found.sh` | Unknown UUID → 404 |
| `library_publish_baseline_invariant.sh` | Two-pointer invariant on publish |
| `library_resource_happy_path.sh` | Teaching resource lifecycle |
| `library_attachment_upload_and_preview.sh` | Attachment upload and preview |
| `library_attachment_validation.sh` | MIME whitelist enforcement |
| `library_preview_unsupported_type.sh` | Unsupported preview MIME → 415 |
| `resource_ownership_enforcement.sh` | Instructor resource ownership scope |
| `academic_course_happy_path.sh` | Course CRUD and versioning |
| `academic_course_validation.sh` | Course code / credit bounds validation |
| `academic_course_unauthorized.sh` | Course writes rejected without capability |
| `academic_prerequisites.sh` | Prerequisite graph, cycle detection |
| `academic_section_happy_path.sh` | Section lifecycle |
| `academic_section_validation.sh` | Section field validation |
| `academic_import_dry_run.sh` | Bulk import dry-run (no mutation) |
| `academic_import_commit.sh` | Bulk import commit (all-or-nothing) |
| `academic_import_unauthorized.sh` | Import blocked without `ImportExport` cap |
| `academic_import_export_format_check.sh` | CSV and XLSX format round-trips |
| `academic_export_scope.sh` | Department-scoped export enforcement |
| `checkin_happy_path.sh` | Check-in creation and retrieval |
| `checkin_duplicate_blocked.sh` | Duplicate within window → 409 |
| `checkin_retry_happy_path.sh` | Retry with valid reason code |
| `checkin_retry_invalid_reason.sh` | Unknown reason code → 422 |
| `checkin_retry_reasons_endpoint.sh` | Reason codes listing |
| `checkin_unauthorized.sh` | Check-in blocked without `CheckinWrite` cap |
| `checkin_masking.sh` | PII masking for non-privileged callers |
| `metric_crud_happy_path.sh` | Metric definition lifecycle |
| `metric_publish_requires_admin.sh` | Publish blocked for non-admin → 403 |
| `metric_lineage_validation.sh` | Invalid lineage ref → 422 |
| `metric_version_dependent_widget_flag.sh` | Widget `verification_needed` flag |
| `dashboard_date_filter.sh` | Date-range filter validation |
| `dashboard_unauthorized_returns_403_or_401.sh` | Dashboard access control |
| `dashboard_masking_instructor_workload.sh` | Workload masking for non-admin |
| `dashboard_derived_from_real_data.sh` | Dashboard panels reflect stored data |
| `report_create_and_run.sh` | Report creation and on-demand run |
| `report_department_scope.sh` | Report listing scoped by department |
| `report_scope_isolation.sh` | Single-object scope enforcement → 403 |
| `report_schedule_lifecycle.sh` | Schedule create / update / delete |
| `retention_policy_update.sh` | Retention policy configuration |
| `retention_execute.sh` | Retention execution (dry-run and live) |
| `encryption_field_masking.sh` | AES-256-GCM field encryption round-trip |

## Helper library (`_common.sh`)

Sourced automatically by every script. Do not invoke it directly.

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `BASE` | `${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1` — base URL for all requests |
| `DEFAULT_PASSWORD` | Seed password (`ChangeMe!Scholarly2026`) |
| `login_as <email> [password]` | Log in and return a bearer token |
| `expect_status <want> <got> <label>` | Assert HTTP status; exit 1 on mismatch |
| `pass <label>` | Print a PASS line |
| `fail <label>` | Print a FAIL line and exit 1 |
| `have_jq` | Returns 0 if `jq` is available |
| `json_field <file> <field>` | Extract a top-level JSON field (uses `jq` or `grep`) |
"}

content.text: The file /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/README.md has been updated successfully.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

This session continued from a prior conversation and covered nine sequential engineering tasks on the "Scholarly" offline-first Rust + Dioxus monorepo:

1. **Frontend navigation auth refactor** — Replace level-based `role_has_access` checks in `main_layout.rs` with an explicit per-route allowlist function `nav_item_allowed(roles: &[Role], route: &AppRoute) -> bool`, using full `Vec<Role>`. Add unit tests for all 6 roles with at least one negative case each.

2. **DB-backed integration tests** — Replace placeholder no-op tests in `backend/tests/api_routes_test.rs` with 5 real authz/scope integration tests using Rocket's local client and a live MySQL connection, gated behind `SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL` env var.

3. **Health endpoint security fix** — Stop leaking raw `sqlx` error strings in degraded health responses; use a static generic message, log the real error server-side with `tracing::warn!`, and add 8 unit tests.

4. **API doc drift fix** — Correct stale "Both routes require AuditRead" wording in `docs/api_surface.md` to accurately describe all three audit log routes and their distinct guards.

5. **README.md contradiction fix** — Remove or rewrite "What Is NOT Implemented Yet" claims about features that are now fully implemented (Auditor role, audit CSV export, check-ins, metrics, reports, retention); add "Current Implementation Status" table.

6. **DB integration test skip guardrail** — Add a `--strict-integration` flag to `run_tests.sh` that fails if `SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL` is absent, plus a visible bordered notice in the default skip path, and expand README "Running Tests" section.

7. **audit_logs.rs comment fix** — Replace stale 7-line module doc block with an accurate authz summary table covering all three routes.

8. **API_tests/README.md documentation** — Full rewrite adding prerequisites, single-script and full-suite invocation paths, `BACKEND_URL` guidance, a table of all 57 test scripts with descriptions, and a helper library reference.

9. **Conversation summary** — Final task: produce this text-only summary.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Dioxus SPA** — `AppRoute` enum via `dioxus-router`, `NavItem` struct, role-aware navigation with `nav_item_allowed`
- **Rocket REST API** — `build_rocket()`, `rocket::local::asynchronous::Client`, `AuthedPrincipal`/`AdminOnly` request guards, `AppResult<T>`, `BinaryDownload` responder
- **RBAC capability matrix** — 6 roles (Admin, Auditor, Librarian, DepartmentHead, Instructor, Viewer), capabilities `AuditRead`/`AuditExport`/`AdminOnly`, `role_allows()` in `authorization.rs`
- **Object-level scope enforcement** — `content_scope()`, `require_object_visible()`, `ScopeFilter` enum, department scope via `creator_department()`
- **sqlx + MySQL** — `MySqlPool`, `init_pool()`, integration tests connecting via `SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL` env var
- **`#[tokio::test]`** — async test functions using tokio runtime (tokio already in `[dependencies]`)
- **Rocket local test client** — `Client::untracked()` vs `Client::tracked()`, `--test-threads=1` to avoid `set_var` races
- **Seed users** — `admin@scholarly.local`, `librarian@scholarly.local`, `depthead@scholarly.local`, `viewer@scholarly.local`, `auditor@scholarly.local`; all share password `ChangeMe!Scholarly2026`; set via bootstrap sentinel `__BOOTSTRAP__`
- **tracing** — `tracing::warn!(error = %db_error, "…")` for structured server-side logging
- **Static vs runtime strings** — `message: Option<&'static str>` as compile-time guarantee that no runtime error text enters the response
- **`_common.sh`** — shared bash helpers: `login_as`, `expect_status`, `pass`, `fail`, `json_field`, `have_jq`, `BASE="${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1"`

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

**`frontend/src/layouts/main_layout.rs`** — Complete rewrite
- Removed `required_role: Option<Role>` from `NavItem` struct
- Removed `role_has_access(user_role: &Role, required: &Option<Role>) -> bool`
- Added explicit `pub fn nav_item_allowed(roles: &[Role], route: &AppRoute) -> bool`:
```rust
pub fn nav_item_allowed(roles: &[Role], route: &AppRoute) -> bool {
    let has = |r: &Role| roles.contains(r);
    match route {
        AppRoute::Dashboard {} | AppRoute::JournalList {} | AppRoute::ResourceList {}
        | AppRoute::CourseList {} => true,
        AppRoute::Imports {} => has(&Role::Admin) || has(&Role::Librarian) || has(&Role::DepartmentHead),
        AppRoute::CheckIn {} => has(&Role::Admin) || has(&Role::DepartmentHead) || has(&Role::Instructor),
        AppRoute::Reports {} => has(&Role::Admin) || has(&Role::Auditor) || has(&Role::Librarian)
            || has(&Role::DepartmentHead) || has(&Role::Instructor),
        AppRoute::Metrics {} => has(&Role::Admin) || has(&Role::Librarian)
            || has(&Role::DepartmentHead) || has(&Role::Instructor),
        AppRoute::AuditLogs {} => has(&Role::Admin) || has(&Role::Auditor),
        AppRoute::AdminSettings {} | AppRoute::RetentionSettings {} => has(&Role::Admin),
        _ => false,
    }
}
```
- Template uses `roles: Vec<Role>` from `snapshot.user.as_ref().map(|u| u.roles.clone()).unwrap_or_default()`
- `primary_role` kept only for topbar badge display
- 14 unit tests: per-role positive/negative cases + multi-role union + empty roles

**`backend/tests/api_routes_test.rs`** — Complete rewrite (5 real tests)
- Skip gate: `fn test_db_url() -> Option<String> { std::env::var("SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL").ok() }`
- Client builder: `async fn build_client(db_url: &str) -> Client` — sets `DATABASE_URL`, calls `build_rocket().await`, returns `Client::untracked`
- Login helper: `async fn login_as(client: &Client, email: &str, password: &str) -> String`
- Auth header builder: `fn bearer(token: &str) -> Header<'static>`
- Test 1: `db_unauthenticated_request_is_rejected_with_401` — no header + malformed header both → 401
- Test 2: `db_non_admin_is_forbidden_on_admin_only_endpoint` — librarian→403, admin→200 on `/audit-logs/verify-chain`
- Test 3: `db_report_schedule_listing_enforces_department_scope` — admin creates report via POST, depthead→403 on schedules, admin→200
- Test 4: `db_audit_export_denied_to_non_exporting_roles` — viewer→403, librarian→403 on `/audit-logs/export.csv`
- Test 5: `db_admin_audit_export_returns_200_with_valid_csv` — admin→200, `text/csv` content-type, `attachment` disposition, correct 10-column header

**`backend/Cargo.toml`** — `[dev-dependencies]` updated:
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
serde_json = "1"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
```

**`backend/src/api/health.rs`** — Security fix + 8 unit tests:
```rust
const DEGRADED_DB_MESSAGE: &str = "database connectivity check failed";

pub struct HealthResponse {
    pub status: &'static str,
    pub database: &'static str,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub message: Option<&'static str>,
}

fn degraded(db_error: &sqlx::Error) -> HealthResponse {
    tracing::warn!(error = %db_error, "health check: database connectivity check failed");
    HealthResponse { status: "degraded", database: "error", message: Some(DEGRADED_DB_MESSAGE) }
}

#[get("/health")]
pub async fn health_check(pool: &State<MySqlPool>) -> Json<HealthResponse> {
    let response = match sqlx::query("SELECT 1").execute(pool.inner()).await {
        Ok(_) => healthy(),
        Err(ref e) => degraded(e),
    };
    Json(response)
}
```
- Test `degraded_message_does_not_contain_sql_or_driver_internals` checks forbidden substrings: `["Access denied", "scholarly_app", "db-host", "password"]`

**`docs/api_surface.md`** — Three targeted edits to audit section:
1. "Both routes require AuditRead" → authz summary table:
```
| Endpoint                                | Guard / Capability | Roles with access  |
|-----------------------------------------|--------------------|---------------------|
| GET /api/v1/audit-logs                  | AuditRead          | Admin, Auditor      |
| GET /api/v1/audit-logs/verify-chain     | AdminOnly guard    | Admin only          |
| GET /api/v1/audit-logs/export.csv       | AuditExport        | Admin only          |
```
2. Masking note: removed "(which, in Phase 2, means nobody)" → "Auditor callers see `actor_email` as `user:<8-hex-prefix-of-sha256>` and `ip_address` as `[REDACTED]` (masking is keyed on `is_admin()`, not role level)"
3. verify-chain description clarified: "Requires the `AdminOnly` request guard (not a capability check — the route itself is restricted to `Role::Admin`)"

**`README.md`** — Six edits:
- Added Auditor to Default Seed Users table: `| Auditor | auditor@scholarly.local | ChangeMe!Scholarly2026 |`
- Phase 2 RBAC claim: "named capabilities across 6 roles (Admin, Librarian, Auditor, DepartmentHead, Instructor, Viewer)"; "AuditRead (Admin and Auditor). Audit log CSV export requires the separate `Capability::AuditExport` (Admin only)"
- Phase 1 note: wrapped as `*(Historical record as of Phase 1: domain business features were stubs at that point; all are now fully implemented through Phase 7.)*`
- Added "Current Implementation Status" section (18-row table)
- Renamed "What Is Implemented (Phase 1+2+3+4)" → "What Is Implemented (Phase 1 – Phase 4 detail)"

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`cargo` not on host or in running containers** — The backend runs as a compiled binary in a slim Debian image with no Rust toolchain. All compilation and test execution was done via `docker run --rm -v .../backend:/app -w /app rust:1.88-bookworm bash -c 'cargo test ...'`.

- **Docker network name** — First attempt used `scholarly_default`; actual network was `repo_scholarly-net`. Fixed by running `docker network ls | grep scholarly` and using the correct name.

- **Background job output not immediately available** — Used `docker wait <container_id>` then `docker logs` to get final output after the compilation finished.

- **Pre-existing failing tests unrelated to health changes** — `course_code_rejects_mixed_case_and_spaces` and `report_format_unknown_db_value_returns_none` were already failing before any changes. Confirmed as pre-existing by inspecting the failure detail (trailing space in course code, enum mapping issue) — neither touched by any changes in this session.

---

5. Problem Solving:

- **`set_var` race in parallel integration tests** — Addressed by documenting `--test-threads=1` as required when `SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL` is set. Since all tests set the same value, the race is harmless but noisy. Using `Client::untracked` avoids drop-warning issues when response bodies are not consumed.

- **`Option<String>` vs `Option<&'static str>` for health message** — Switching to `&'static str` makes it a compile-time guarantee that no dynamically-constructed string (i.e., error text) can enter the `message` field of `HealthResponse`.

- **False-green risk from skipped integration tests** — The existing approach (tests silently pass with zero assertions) cannot be detected from `cargo test` output alone. Solved through the `run_tests.sh` skip notice (visible at script level, not captured by cargo) and the `--strict-integration` failure mode for CI.

- **Non-hierarchical access patterns impossible in level-based nav** — The old `role_has_access` using `level() >= threshold` could not represent "Auditor sees AuditLogs but not CheckIn" since Auditor (90) > Instructor (40) who does see CheckIn. The explicit match-per-route function solves this exactly.
